zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Norman Leslie Of That Ilk於大約1246年出生在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾時，他的父親Norino Leslie 4th of Leslie, Constable of Inverurie58歲，母親Catherine Mure22歲。他於1288年與Lady Eva Morthach, Lady of Rothes在蘇格蘭結婚。他去世於1317年之前。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Norman Leslie Of That Ilk于大约1246年出生在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾时，他的父亲Norino Leslie 4th of Leslie, Constable of Inverurie58岁，母亲Catherine Mure22岁。他于1288年与Lady Eva Morthach, Lady of Rothes在苏格兰结婚。他去世于1317年之前。

nameMeanings.name: Leslie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from a barony in Aberdeenshire, which is first recorded c. 1180 in the form Lesslyn, of obscure origin. Leslie in Fife is said to be named for this place; in some cases the surname may come from there.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Lecelin, a double diminutive of Lece. See Leece and Lesley .

History: The Scottish Clan Leslie claims descent from Bartolf, a Hungarian nobleman who came to Scotland in 1068 or 1070 in the retinue of Queen Margaret. She was an English princess who had been born and brought up in Hungary and who went on to marry King Malcolm III of Scotland. Members of the clan have held several titles in the peerage of Scotland, including Earl of Rothes (from 1437).

variants.name: Leece
meanings: English: from a medieval female personal name, Lece, a short form of Lettice (from Latin Laetitia, meaning ‘happiness, gaiety’).
English: variant of   Lees  .
Irish: shortened form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Íosa, Manx Mac Guilley Yeesey ‘son of Giolla Íosa (devotee or servant of Jesus)’. Ballaleece (‘Leece's farm’) in Kirk German was the family's main seat.

variants.name: Lesslie
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Leslie  .

variants.name: Lesley
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Leslie  .

variants.name: Laslie
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Leslie  .

variants.name: Lessley
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Leslie  .

variants.name: Lasley
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Leslie  .

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: That
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably an Americanized form of German Thiet(e), a variant of Thiede .
English (mainly Huntingdonshire): probably a variant of   Tate  .

variants.name: Thiede
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Erwin, Frieda, Helmut, Herta, Klaus, Kurt, Ralf.
North German: from a pet form of the personal name   Dietrich  .

variants.name: Teate
meanings: Probably an Americanized form of German Thiet (see Teat ) or Thiede .
English: variant of   Teat  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Ilk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Mariusz, Stanislaw, Tadeusz, Zygmunt, Andrzej, Bogdan, Boguslaw, Casimir, Krzysztof, Stanislawa, Boleslaw, Darek.

Polish: from the vocabulary word wilk ‘wolf’, probably applied as a nickname for someone thought to resemble a wolf or connected with wolves in some way. Alternatively, from an Old Slavic personal name based on vlъkъ ‘wolf’.
English (Staffordshire): from Middle English Wilke, a pet form of William and probably a shortening of Wilkin . The usage was very likely Flemish in origin.

variants.name: Wilczak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Andrzej, Danuta, Stanislawa, Witold, Wojciech.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name   Wilk  .

variants.name: Wilkes
meanings: English (West Midlands): either a variant of Wilk , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s, or from the Middle English personal name Wilcus, an uncommon pet form of a personal name in Wil-. The suffix is a double diminutive -k + -us, the latter being used with Old English female names (compare Aldous ). So Wilcus may also be a pet form of an Old English female name such as Wilburg. Alternatively, it may have been used with Norman personal names of either gender, for example as a pet form of William . If Wilcus became an inherited surname it would quickly have become merged with Wilkes. Wilcus may also have been absorbed into Wildgoose .
Dutch and North German: patronymic from the personal name Wilke, a pet form of Will.

variants.name: Wilkosz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Henryk.
Polish: derivative of   Wilk  .

variants.name: William
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

variants.name: Wilczek
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Stanislaw, Casimir, Ewa, Krzystof, Miroslaw, Zosia.  German Gunther, Natascha.
Polish: from a diminutive of the personal name Wilk , from wilk ‘wolf’.

nameMeanings.name: Norman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic origin, from nord ‘north’ + man ‘man’, i.e. ‘Norseman’. This name was in use in England before the Conquest, and was reinforced by its use among the Norman invaders themselves. The Normans were the inhabitants of Normandy in northern France, whose name is a reference to the Vikings who took control of the region in the 9th century. In the 11th and 12th centuries they achieved remarkable conquests, including not only Britain but also Sicily, southern Italy, and Antioch. In the Scottish Highlands it is used as the Anglicized equivalent of Tormod .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Norman Leslie Of That Ilk was born about 1246, in Aberdeenshire, Scotland, his father, Norino Leslie 4th of Leslie, Constable of Inverurie, was 58 and his mother, Catherine Mure, was 22. He married Lady Eva Morthach, Lady of Rothes in 1288, in Scotland. He died before 1317.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Norman Leslie Of That Ilk est né environ 1246, à Aberdeenshire, Écosse, son père, Norino Leslie 4th of Leslie, Constable of Inverurie, avait 58 ans et sa mère, Catherine Mure, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Lady Eva Morthach, Lady of Rothes en 1288, à Écosse. Il est décédé avant 1317.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Norman Leslie Of That Ilk um 1246 in Aberdeenshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Norino Leslie 4th of Leslie, Constable of Inverurie 58 und seine Mutter Catherine Mure 22. Er hat 1288 in Scotland Lady Eva Morthach, Lady of Rothes geheiratet. Er starb vor 1317.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Norman Leslie Of That Ilk è nato il circa 1246 nel Aberdeenshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Norino Leslie 4th of Leslie, Constable of Inverurie, aveva 58 anni e il suo madre, Catherine Mure, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Lady Eva Morthach, Lady of Rothes nel 1288, nel Scotland. Morì prima del 1317.
